arts.ttl: Champions League Liveblog: Bayer Leverkusen-Milan, Inter-Crvena Zvezda
Inter eyeing defender Jonathan Tah on free transfer
arts.sum: Bayer Leverkusen defender Jonathan Tah has become a hot topic in the transfer market as his contract nears its end. The 27-year-old German international has confirmed he won’t be renewing with the Bundesliga club, sparking interest from several top European teams. Calciomercato report that Inter Milan has been closely monitoring Tah’s situation for some time. The Nerazzurri are looking to bolster their defensive options, potentially as replacements for aging center-backs Francesco Acerbi and Stefan de Vrij. Tah’s potential availability on a free transfer makes him an attractive prospect for Inter, who are always on the lookout for cost-effective signings. arts.ttl: DFL launches investigation against Bayern player who insulted Leverkusen boss
arts.sum: After Sven Ulreich was caught on camera insulting Bayer Leverkusen boss Simon Rolfes during Saturday's game at the Allianz Arena, the German Football League (DFL) has launched an investigation against the Bayern Munich goalkeeper.
Joshua Kimmich believes Bayern Munich made Bayer Leverkusen look "harmless" in Saturday's 1-1 draw.

arts.ttl: Champions League | Bayer Leverkusen v AC Milan: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: As Milan prepare to face Bayer Leverkusen in their second Champions League match, the German champions are ready to present a formidable challenge at the BayArena. Coming off a 1-1 draw against Bayern Munich in the Bundesliga, Xabi Alonso’s side is looking to maintain their strong form in European competition. Alonso is expected to stick with his preferred 3-4-2-1 formation, likely fielding the same starting eleven that faced Bayern Munich. Here’s how Bayer Leverkusen is expected to line up: In goal, Hradecky will continue as the last line of defense.
